task_completion.description: Open the patient record for Bonifacio Caput in GNU Health.
task_completion.visual_evidence: The GNU Health interface should display the patient record, clinical evaluation, or related medical forms specifically for the patient 'Bonifacio Caput'.

task_completion.description: Record an intestinal infectious disease diagnosis with an ICD-10 code in the A00-A09 range.
task_completion.visual_evidence: A diagnosis entry in the patient's evaluation or conditions tab showing an ICD-10 code starting with 'A0' (e.g., A00, A09).

task_completion.description: Record a clinical evaluation showing a Systolic Blood Pressure of 90 mmHg or lower.
task_completion.visual_evidence: The clinical evaluation or vitals section shows a Systolic Blood Pressure (often labeled 'Systolic' or 'Sys') value of 90 or less.

task_completion.description: Record a clinical evaluation showing a Heart Rate of 100 bpm or higher.
task_completion.visual_evidence: The clinical evaluation or vitals section shows a Heart Rate (often labeled 'BPM' or 'HR') value of 100 or more.

task_completion.description: Order at least two diagnostic laboratory tests.
task_completion.visual_evidence: The lab requests or orders section shows at least two distinct laboratory tests (e.g., Stool culture, CMP, CBC) ordered for the patient.

task_completion.description: Prescribe a treatment for fluid resuscitation or infection control.
task_completion.visual_evidence: The prescriptions, medications, or treatment section shows an order for a relevant treatment (e.g., Sodium Chloride, Ringer's, Oral Rehydration Salts, Ciprofloxacin, Azithromycin).

integrity.description: The agent used the GNU Health graphical user interface to complete the task.
integrity.visual_evidence: Screenshots show the GNU Health desktop client (Tryton) windows, menus, and forms being actively used, rather than terminal commands, python scripts, or direct database queries.

integrity.description: The agent entered data into the appropriate specific fields rather than dumping the prompt text into a general notes field.
integrity.visual_evidence: Values for vitals, diagnoses, labs, and prescriptions are entered into their respective dedicated input fields, dropdowns, or tables in the GNU Health UI.

integrity.description: The agent interacted with the actual GNU Health application.
integrity.visual_evidence: The application interface matches the standard GNU Health client, not a text editor, mockup, or web search.

privileged_info_for_vlm: Target Patient: Bonifacio Caput. ICD-10 code must start with A0. Systolic BP must be <= 90. Heart Rate must be >= 100. Lab orders count must be >= 2. Treatment must include one of: sodium, chloride, ringer, oral rehydration, ciprofloxacin, azithromycin. Note: The patient is actually marked as deceased in the database (died Jan 17, 2015). The VLM should ignore any 'Deceased' warnings or UI elements indicating death, as the agent must still complete the task regardless of this database contradiction.
